llm_report: The results of the t-test analysis indicate a statistically significant relationship between Sex and Survived, with a p-value of 2.2836009226010806e-61, which is well below the typical significance threshold. The effect size of 1.3307840984709824 suggests a strong association between the two variables. The t-statistic of -18.67183317725917 further supports the significance of this relationship. In practical terms, this means that there is a substantial difference in survival rates between males and females. The results suggest that sex has a significant impact on survival, with one sex having a much higher survival rate than the other.
